EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A upper level disturbance moved across the region in the morning. Temperatures were well below freezing and snow showers accumulated on surfaces. Snow became heavier across the Washington DC metro and Southern Maryland.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of two inches or more were measured at Adams Morgan.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the Mid-Atlantic from west to east. Hot and humid conditions were ahead of the frontal boundary and led to an unstable environment. Severe thunderstorms were capable of damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down near the intersection of 1st Street and U Street Northeast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The eastern half of the United States was under cyclonic flow and moisture from the Atlantic and Gulf continued to move into the Mid Atlantic. A warm front moved northward and showers and thunderstorms broke out across the area. Heavy rain produced flash flooding and rapid rises on streams and creeks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The stream gauge at Rock Creek in Washington DC reached flood stage. This event peaked to 11.98 feet. At 12 feet, water completely covered the Beach Drive Bridge at Milkhouse Ford in Rock Creek Park between Military Road and Bingham Drive. Virtually all of Rock Creek Park was flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was over the western Atlantic Ocean and southerly flow brought hot and humid conditions into the Mid-Atlantic leading to a unstable atmosphere. Showers and thunderstorms moved across the region in the afternoon and evening and some thunderstorms were capable of damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down on 28th Street NW and Q Street NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was over the western Atlantic Ocean and southerly flow brought hot and humid conditions into the Mid-Atlantic leading to a unstable atmosphere. Showers and thunderstorms moved across the region in the afternoon and evening and some thunderstorms were capable of damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down at 3rd Street NE and R Street NE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved across the Mid Atlantic in the morning. A jet axis coincided with the disturbance and snow showers moved across the region. The most accumulation occurred east of the Blue Ridge.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of an inch or more was measured at surrounding locations during rush hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep upper level trough tapped into Gulf and Atlantic moisture which led to heavy rain across the Mid Atlantic. Tropical-like conditions resulted in showers and thunderstorms to persist before a cold front moved through later in the day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The river at Rock Creek reached flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper low moved into the Mid Atlantic and mid level forcing led to snow across portions of the Mid Atlantic. Wrap around moisture around the surface low and dynamics from the upper low led to snow fall accumulation across Washington DC metro and Southern Maryland.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of 2 inches or more were measured at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wedge of high pressure extended southwest along the Appalachian Mountains. Low pressure approached the Mid Atlantic from the Tennessee Valley and warm air overran colder air at the surface resulting in freezing rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ice accumulation of a trace was reported at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A upper level trough stretched from Southern Canada to the Gulf Coast and an abundant amount of moisture was present across the Mid-Atlantic. A surface low was moving across the region in the overnight hours and showers and thunderstorms formed along the attendant cold front and surface trough. Onshore flow led to increased tidal anomalies and therefore coastal flooding in Washington DC.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The river gauge at the Washington DC Southwest Waterfront and Maine Avenue reached moderate flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Freshwater moved down the Potomac River towards the Washington DC waterfront due to an abundant amount of rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The river gauge at the Washington DC Southwest Waterfront and Maine Avenue reached moderate flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Ridging across the area led to clear and calm conditions and radiational cooling led to temperatures to drop to saturation quickly. Dense fog formed quickly for areas east of the Blue Ridge.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility of a quarter mile or less was measured at Reagan National.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough moved into the region while low pressure developed south of the Mid Atlantic. Upper level dynamics led to moderate to heavy snow to move into the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of five inches or more were measured at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure resided across the east coast while a warm front was located across the Carolinas. Moist air continued to move into the Mid Atlantic as showers intensified ahead of the warm front. Heavy rain produced flooding in parts of the Washington DC and Baltimore Metro Areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rock Creek exceeded its flood stage of 7 feet. There were numerous road closures reported and park areas were flooded throughout the afternoon. The river went above flood stage at 10:50 AM EDT and transitioned to flood. The peak was at 9.89 feet at 6:30 PM EDT and went below flood stage at 11:10 PM EDT.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A lee trough developed across the Mid-Atlantic that led to the development of showers and thunderstorms. The atmosphere was unstable and scattered storms produced damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down on homes at the 3500 Block of Park Place NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure moved past the Mid Atlantic from the Carolinas. High pressure to the north fed freezing temperatures to the region resulting in snow to accumulate across the region. Snowfall occurred during the morning rush for the Baltimore/Washington DC and Interstate 95 corridor.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulation of 2 inches or more was measured at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two areas of low pressure formed south of the Mid Atlantic. Dry and cold air at the surface led to precipitation to quickly change to snow. Heavy snow fell across the region with a confined area of greater than 10 inches across the Central Foothills.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of greater than 5 inches fell at Reagan National.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak disturbance crossed the Mid Atlantic while a wedge of high pressure was at the surface. Precipitation that fell melted aloft and froze on contact.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ice accumulations of a trace or more were measured at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure tracked across the Mid Atlantic and led to accumulating snow with the highest amounts from Northern Virginia to East-Central Maryland. Low pressure quickly moved off the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulation of 3 inches was measured at Washington.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure tracked up the Appalachian mountains and southerly flow ahead of the system led to warm air overrunning cold air at the surface. This led to freezing rain and ice accumulation across the Mid Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ice accumulation of a trace or more was measured at surrounding locations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front crossed the region as low pressure passed across the south of the Mid Atlantic and heavy snow moved across the region. Temperatures dropped from north to south and precipitation changed from rain to sleet/freezing rain to snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall accumulations of five inches were measured at Washington.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The eastern half of the United States was under cyclonic flow and moisture from the Atlantic and Gulf continued to move into the Mid Atlantic. A warm front moved northward and showers and thunderstorms broke out across the area. Heavy rain produced flash flooding and rapid rises on streams and creeks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The stream gauge at Rock Creek in Washington DC reached flood stage. This event peaked at 11.98 feet. At 12 feet, water completely covered the Beach Drive Bridge at Milkhouse Ford in Rock Creek Park between Military Road and Bingham Drive. Virtually all of Rock Creek Park is flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A frontal boundary was located across the region. Warm and humid conditions returned to the Mid Atlantic slow moving showers and thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall in localized areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was high water reported flowing across 295 South of 50 just inside DC limits.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a large tree down on power lines on Klingle Street NW at Foxhall Road NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a large tree uprooted in the 4500 Block of Klingle Street NW.

Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree reported down on Rockwood Parkway NW at Glenbrook Road NW.

Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree reported down at 3rd Street NW and Rock Creek Church NW.

Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree reported down on 295 near Benning Road NE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was reported down on the 3300 Block of Alden Place NE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.

Low pressure moved up the east coast and approached the Mid Atlantic. High pressure was located across New England and fed cold air into the region. Heavy snow fell across most parts of the Mid Atlantic with the highest amounts near the Mason Dixon line where mid level forcing led to a heavy band.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow accumulations of 8 inches were measured at Washington.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Persistent southerly flow led to moderate coastal flooding at times of the maximum astronomical high tide along the western shores of the MD Chesapeake Bay and Washington DC waterfront on the Tidal Potomac.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water approached the intersection of V Street and Half Street in Buzzard Point in Washington DC. This event continued into May and has an end time of 10:22 EST May 1 2014.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Persistent southerly flow led to moderate coastal flooding at times of the maximum astronomical high tide along the western shores of the MD Chesapeake Bay and Washington DC waterfront on the Tidal Potomac.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate flooding occurred at Georgetown in Washington DC. Water began to cover the boardwalk at Washington Harbour. Water also is out of the banks in a few low spots between the mouth of Rock Creek and the 14th Street Bridge. Washington Harbour will begin to flood if floodgates are not in place. This event continued into May and has an end time of 10:02 EST May 1 2014.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree reported down on a house causing structural damage in the 3100 Block of Arizona Avenue NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Mid-Atlantic was in between a cold front approaching form the Appalachian Mountains and Hurricane Arthur off the Delaware Coast. Warm and humid conditions were across the region and showers and thunderstorms led to heavy rain. Unstable conditions led to severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees and wires were reported down in the 3300 Block of Baker Street NE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Increasing and persistent southeast flow ahead of an approaching cold front combining with astronomically high tides resulted in anomalies around 2 feet and widespread minor flooding with isolated areas reaching moderate levels.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Washington Harbor called to inform that water was coming up through the storm drains around the time of high tide. Tidal gauge WASD2 reported moderate tidal levels.
